So OpenAI just casually dropped superhuman AGI like it's a software update, and within 24 hours the global economy is having what can only be described as a complete nervous breakdown. Because apparently when you can automate literally everything, investors suddenly remember that humans need jobs to buy things.

Universities are straight-up giving up and telling students to go home because why would you need four years of college when an AI can teach you quantum physics during your morning coffee break. The entire higher education industrial complex just became as relevant as a rotary phone repair shop.

NATO's scrambling for an emergency meeting because apparently nobody thought to game out what happens when every military strategy ever conceived becomes obsolete in a single Tuesday afternoon. Turns out geopolitical stability was built on a house of cards labeled 'humans are still the smartest thing around.'

Medical researchers are having the best month of their entire careers as AGI is basically speedrunning the cure for cancer, diabetes, and probably that weird thing where you get hiccups for three days straight. Clinical trials that used to take decades are now getting done faster than Amazon Prime delivery.

Hollywood writers are on strike because AGI wrote the next Marvel Cinematic Universe in about six seconds, complete with character arcs, plot twists, and probably better dialogue than anything they've produced since 2008. The AI even figured out how to make superhero movies good again, which honestly feels personal.

Dating apps have become so terrifyingly efficient at matching people that they're basically playing god with human relationships, predicting soulmates with the accuracy of a Swiss watch. Love is now an algorithm, and somehow it's working better than whatever the hell we were doing before.

Some burrito shop in San Francisco charged FORTY-SEVEN DOLLARS for a burrito because an AI told them it was the 'optimal flavor profile' and I swear to god if artificial superintelligence leads to premium pricing on basic Mexican food I'm going to lose my absolute mind over this dystopian nightmare where even our lunch is being gentrified by robots.
